violations.text: Molluscan Shellfish oysters, little necks located in the walk in cooler did not have the proper identification tags. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain with the Molluscan Shellstock until the container is empty. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2026

The door to the outside, located by the back receiving entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2026

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the upstairs dishwasher have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. NFCS must be maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 01/15/2026

The discharge from the soda gun at the small bar is draining into the three bay at the bar. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2026
Areas of the floor in the kitchen, and behind the large bar were observed with broken tiles. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine, can opener, deli slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice Disposed of Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2024

Marinara Sauce, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Sauce was observed at 85 DF. Sauce was prepared night before. Sauce was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2024

Upstairs reach in was observed held at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Whipped dessert 48 DF in unit was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of Chef, Marinara Sauce was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/11/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2024

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2024
The floor behind the bar area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
